rulings.Case Title: LVNV FUNDING LLC VS. MABLE DING
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, August 25, 2014, Line 3, DEFENDANT MABLE DING's MOTION to SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT. Granted. (CCP sec. 473(d).) The default and default judgment are set aside. Defendant has established that she was never served with the summons and the complaint, and therefore, the Court never obtained personal jurisdiction. (See CCP sec. 410.50; Dill v. Berquist Construction Co. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 1426, 1439, "In the absence of a voluntary submission to the authority of the court, compliance with the statutes governing service of process is essential to establish that court's personal jurisdiction over a defendant.") "When a court lacks jurisdiction in a fundamental sense, an ensuing judgment is void, and thus vulnerable to direct or collateral attack at any time." (Strathvale Holdings v. E.B.H. (2005) 126 Cal.App.4th 1241, 1249.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ROSARIO M. O'CONNOR et al VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.'S Notice Of Rescheduled Hearing On Motion To Compel
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 25, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.'S Hearing On Motion To Compel. Pro Tem Judge Frank Conway, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: MOTION TO COMPEL GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. WITHIN 10 CALENDAR DAYS OF ENTRY OF THE ORDER ON THIS MOTION, ALL PLAINTIFFS ARE TO SERVE, WITHOUT RELEVANCE AND PRIVACY OBJECTIONS AND WITH A PRIVILEGE LOG AS TO REMAINING OBJECTIONS, FURTHER RESPONSES TO DOCUMENT REQUEST NOS. 20 (LIMITED TO 5 YEARS BEFORE PLAINTIFFS' INVESTMENT IN TIME DEPOSIT CERTIFICATES), 21, AND SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NO. 10; THE MOTION IS MOOT AS TO DOCUMENT REQUEST NOS. 24 AND 26; DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH CCP 2023.040; PLAINTIFF'S SANCTIONS REQUEST DENIED SINCE DEFENDANT'S MOTION WAS PARTIALLY SUCCESSFUL. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to FConway@partonsell.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CENTER FOR ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH VS. FOOD MARKET MANAGEMENT, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Safeway Inc.S Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, August 25, 2014, Line 8, PLAINTIFF CENTER FOR ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH's Motion To Compel Defendant Safeway Inc.S Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions. Granted. All General Objections are stricken. (See CCP sec. 2031.210-240.) As to the Special Interrogatories, all objections are overruled. Defendant shall serve a verified response to Interrogatories #1-56 no later than Sept. 15, 2014. As to the Request for Production of Documents, all objections are overruled. Defendant shall serve a verified response and produce documents to Requests #1-24 and 26 no later than September 15, 2014. Any document withheld on the claim of privilege must be listed in a privilege log pursuant to CCP sec. 2031.240. Defendant need not list any attorney-client privilege document that was created after Safeway was served with the lawsuit. As to the Requests for Admission, all objections are overruled. Defendant shall serve a verified response to Requests #1-25 no later than September 15, 2014. As to the Form Interrogatories, all objections are overruled. Defendant shall serve a verified response to Form Interrogatories # 1.1, 3.7, 15.1, and 17.1 no later than September 15, 2014. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is granted. The court finds that defendant Safeway failed to meet its obligations under CCP 2030.210-240, CCP 2031.210-240 and CCP 2033.210-240 in responding to discovery. See Clement v. Alegre (2009) 177 CA4th 1277. The court further finds that defendant Safeway failed to meet and confer in good faith prior to the July 29, 2014 hearing. See Townsend v. Superior Court (1998) 61 CA 4th 1431. Sanctions in the amount of $3560 for attorneys' fees and the cost for filing the motion to be made payable to Center for Environmental Health and their attorneys of record, Lexington Law Group to be paid within 30 days of the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JHONA MATHEWS VS. THE ARCHDIOCESE OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Protective Order Re Excessive Interrogatories And Requests For Admissions; Ntc Of Mtn For Sanctions In The Amt Of $2465
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 25, 2014, Line 2, DEFENDANT MONSIGNOR JAMES TARANTINO Motion For Protective Order Re Excessive Interrogatories And Requests For Admissions; Motion For Sanctions In The Amount Of $2465. Pro Tem Judge Frank Conway, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER GRANTED IN PART. DEFENDANT TARANTINO SHALL BE ORDERED TO RESPOND EITHER TO PLAINTIFF'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE, OR TO REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS, SET ONE, BUT NOT BOTH, ONLY TO THE EXTENT THE SUBJECT OF BOTH SETS OF DISCOVERY OVERLAPS. DEFENDANT TARANTINO'S RELATIONSHIP WITH, AND KNOWLEDGE OF, DEFENDANT MCGLAUGHLIN'S BACKGROUND, CHARACTER, AND ACTIVITIES IS RELEVANT AND DISCOVERABLE; BOTH PARTIES' SANCTIONS REQUESTS ARE DENIED SINCE THEY ACTED WITH SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION; COUNSEL SHOULD PREPARE FOR DISCUSSION AT THE HEARING A TABLE SHOWING INTERROGATORIES AND CORRESPONDING REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION THAT IDENTIFY OVERLAPPING SUBJECTS. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to FConway@partonsell.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BIRITE RESTAURANT SUPPLY INC., VS. BETH'S COMMUNITY KITCHEN LLC, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, August 25, 2014, Line 13, PLAINTIFF BIRITE RESTAURANT SUPPLY INC.'s APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER. Off calendar. A Notice of the Right to Attach Order hearing must be served on the defendant at least 16 court days before the hearing. (CCP secs. 484.040, 1005(a)(1) & (b).) Further, substitute service is not deemed complete until the 10th day after mailing. (CCP sec. 415.20.) The proof of service states that the motion was served by substitute service on August 5, 2014, and therefore insufficient notice was provided. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EHG)
